content: IP allowlist storage options: (1) Add text[] column to user_preferences table (simplest, co-located with preferences, avoid new table). (2) Separate ip_allowlist table (more normalized). Given Neon HTTP driver has no transaction support and user_preferences already has unique userId, adding ipAllowlist as text array (jsonb or text[]) to user_preferences is cleanest. Pattern: db.update(userPreferences).set({ipAllowlist: entries, updatedAt}).where(eq(userId)). IPAL-04 (immediate effect) means no in-memory cache on the server — always read from DB on each request. drizzle-orm pg-core supports text('...').array() for text[] columns.
